headlines.title: Watch: Highlights as England stun France 6-4 in third-place play-off
headlines.description: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

headlines.title: Burnham to scrap digital ID to focus on cost of living in first major policy pledge
headlines.description: A spokesperson for the incoming prime minister says his government would “put its focus where people need it right now”.

headlines.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
headlines.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

headlines.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
headlines.description: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

headlines.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Kyiv
headlines.description: One death is reported in what President Zelensky calls "one of the most massive ballistic attacks" on Ukraine's capital.

headlines.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
headlines.description: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?

headlines.title: Best England team for a long time? That's a fact, says Rice
headlines.description: Midfielder Declan Rice says "this is the best England group for a very long time" after they secure a third-placed finish at the World Cup.

headlines.title: 'A 10-goal thriller': England fans react to 6-4 win over France
headlines.description: England's victory means the team finished third and secured their best World Cup result since 1966.

headlines.title: Over to you, Messi - Mbappe leads Golden Boot race for the ages
headlines.description: Kylian Mbappe leads the Golden Boot race by two goals but Lionel Messi could yet overtake him in the World Cup final.

headlines.title: Why the half-time show may be a taste of things to come
headlines.description: The 2026 World Cup's spectacle is creating debate about the place of music in sport - and how it could infiltrate other tournaments.

headlines.title: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
headlines.description: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

headlines.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
headlines.description: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

headlines.title: Why some women are being driven out of the workplace by an illness
headlines.description: Three women describe how endometriosis has affected their careers, as an inquiry takes place.

headlines.title: Can Burnham's casual style survive contact with No 10?
headlines.description: When Burnham becomes PM, he'll find it harder to wear the wardrobe that's central to his political brand.

headlines.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
headlines.description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

headlines.title: Parts of UK to get break in heatwave but dry spell to continue
headlines.description: Cooler temperatures are on the way but the long, dry summer continues for most.

headlines.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
headlines.description: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Messiah without a mandate' and 'attack of the Argie ants'
headlines.description: Today's papers spotlight Andy Burnham on the eve of him becoming Britain's new prime minister.

headlines.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
headlines.description: US forces target Iranian sites for the eighth night in a row, while Iran says it fired drones at the US military in Kuwait.

headlines.title: Record numbers of honours for working class heroes
headlines.description: Record numbers of people from poorer backgrounds are now receiving awards.

headlines.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
headlines.description: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

headlines.title: Ferry carrying 116 passengers sinks off Guyana coast, authorities say
headlines.description: Eight passengers have so far been rescued after the vessel capsized between Georgetown and Port Kaituma.

headlines.title: 'I had stage four cancer at 14, now I'm a doctor'
headlines.description: Ellie Waters-Barnes survived an aggressive stage four cancer and underwent 18 months of treatment.

headlines.title: Escaped big cat found in plant pot after two-month search
headlines.description: It is thought to have escaped from the city and travelled about four miles into the countryside.

headlines.title: The Rock hires out Suffolk cinema for 200 school pupils
headlines.description: Hollywood star fulfils promise of Moana screening for children at St Gregory Primary School.

headlines.title: Bananas! Surprise as back garden plant bears fruit
headlines.description: Stephen Hind believes the current hot temperatures and climate change have encouraged the fruit.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Andrew Marr on Burnham's Plan For Number 10
headlines.description: What will Andy Burnham do when he becomes Prime Minister?

headlines.title: Will Trump's primetime speech help him challenge the midterm elections?
headlines.description: Join Americast for insights and analysis on what's happening inside Trump's White House.

headlines.title: What Has the BBC Ever Done for Me?
headlines.description: Watch to find out

headlines.title: Best England team for a long time? That's a fact, says Rice
headlines.description: Midfielder Declan Rice says "this is the best England group for a very long time" after they secure a third-placed finish at the World Cup.

headlines.title: England beat France in 10-goal thriller to win third-place play-off
headlines.description: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

headlines.title: England's discipline 'verging on comical' - Dawson
headlines.description: Matt Dawson described England's discipline as "comical" and says they have a problem to address after receiving four yellow cards in the 31-24 win over Argentina.

headlines.title: Rapp, 26, dies after being hit by car while cycling
headlines.description: American boxer Hannah Rapp dies aged 26 after being hit by a car while cycling in Texas.

headlines.title: How did England players rate during the World Cup?
headlines.description: BBC Sport England reporter Alex Howell assess how the Three Lions' players fared in North America.

headlines.title: Over to you, Messi - Mbappe leads Golden Boot race for the ages
headlines.description: Kylian Mbappe leads the Golden Boot race by two goals but Lionel Messi could yet overtake him in the World Cup final.
